threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still avoid the Dalston Junction steps, thinking they'll take you somewhere nice. Just wait till you discover the shortcuts all the locals use.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week; the driver serenaded us with '80s hits while navigating those potholes. Best ride ever!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: First time I saw a group of kids scootering past a street art mural while an older man sold homemade jam from a cart. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find late-night kebab shops blasting techno, where the smell of grilled meat mingles with thumping bass. Pure Kreuzberg vibes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission if they can spot the difference between a taco truck’s carne asada and that frozen stuff from the grocery store.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy with a giant inflatable taco trying to board the 5-Fulton at Civic Center. Muni drivers didn’t bat an eye, just waved him on.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just grab a quick bite at any bar, but the tapas rush hour leaves no room for casual dining. It's a whole event.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they order their coffee—still asking for 'with milk' instead of a cortado. Classic rookie mistake.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look up when the tube goes by instead of just ignoring it like everyone else. The first few weeks, you felt like a tourist.
Last week on the N35, a guy serenaded the whole bus with a ukulele, and not one person filmed it. Just a late-night Brixton moment.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw an artist painting a mural while kids played football in the street—no one cared if it was messy, just life happening.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find spontaneous street art tours led by locals who just finished their morning coffee. No schedule, just vibes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a local knows to pack an umbrella on a sunny day—because the fog rolls in fast and turns the pavement slick by evening.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Heard the bus driver serenading riders with a smooth jazz tune while we waited for the dog on the tracks to clear. Classic San Francisco moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just the park and Gaudí. They miss the little plazas where real life happens, like Plaça del Sol at dusk.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their reluctance to squeeze into packed metros during rush hour, sticking to the edges and looking panicked.
